name: Moonshine
tagline: On-device speech recognition built for real-time, low-latency voice apps
metaDescription: Moonshine Voice is an open-source, on-device speech toolkit for real-time transcription, TTS, and voice agents across Python, mobile, and edge devices.
overview: Moonshine Voice is an open-source toolkit for building real-time voice applications. Everything runs on the device itself, so there's no account, API key, or cloud round-trip needed, which keeps responses fast and audio private. The models are trained for live streaming, doing much of the recognition work while the user is still speaking.

It's aimed at developers who want speech-to-text, text-to-speech, speaker identification, command recognition, and conversational agents from a single library. The same toolkit runs across Python, iOS, Android, macOS, Linux, Windows, Raspberry Pi, and other small devices.

In the Speech & Audio category, Moonshine positions itself as an edge-friendly alternative to larger cloud models like Whisper. Its model sizes range from a 245M-parameter streaming model down to tiny 26MB models for constrained hardware, so it fits both desktops and small embedded boards.
features: Runs fully on-device with no account, credit card, or API key required
Optimized for low-latency live streaming, processing speech while the user is still talking
One library covers transcription, text-to-speech, speaker diarization, command recognition, and conversational agents
Cross-platform: Python, iOS, Android, macOS, Linux, Windows, Raspberry Pi, and wearables
Model sizes from a 245M-parameter streaming model down to 26MB models for constrained devices
Multilingual STT and TTS, including English, Spanish, Mandarin, Japanese, Korean, and more

gettingStarted.intro: The fastest way to try Moonshine is the Python package, which can transcribe your microphone in real time. Example apps for iOS, Android, macOS, Windows, and Raspberry Pi are published on GitHub Releases.

steps.heading: Install the Python package
steps.body: Install Moonshine Voice from PyPI with pip.

steps.heading: Transcribe your microphone
steps.body: Listen to the microphone and print updates to the transcript as they come in.

steps.heading: Recognize spoken commands
steps.body: Listen for user-defined action phrases like "Turn on the lights", using semantic matching so natural language variations are recognized.

steps.heading: Synthesize speech (TTS)
steps.body: Convert text to speech and play it back.

useCases: Add real-time, private voice transcription to a desktop or mobile app without sending audio to the cloud
Build offline voice command and intent recognition for smart-home or IoT devices
Run speech-to-text on constrained hardware like a Raspberry Pi using small on-device models
Create conversational voice agents that combine transcription, command handling, and text-to-speech in one library
